title: Malaxis arisanensis
taxonomy: Malaxis arisanensis is classified in the kingdom Plantae, further divided into the clade Angiosperms, the superorder Zygomeris, the order Asparagales, the family Orchidaceae, and the subfamily Orchidoideae. Malaxis arisanensis is a species of orchid, specifically belonging to the genus Malaxis. The genus Malaxis itself is part of the subtribe Malaxinae and the tribe Cranichideae. The classification of Malaxis arisanensis also places it in the family Orchidaceae, which is commonly referred to as the orchid family. Malaxis arisanensis' classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships among other flowering plants.

intro: Malaxis arisanensis is a species of orchid, specifically a type of terrestrial orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Eastern medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Malaxis arisanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Malaxis arisanensis include tea infusions and capsules, which are often used to treat colds, fever, and skin irritations.
